series_name: The Plan of Salvation for Kids
introduction:
Where did you come from? Why are you here? What happens when you die?

These are the biggest questions anyone can ask. And God has answered every one of them. The plan of salvation is His answer -- a perfect plan, perfectly just, perfectly merciful, and personally designed for you.

This book takes you through the entire plan from beginning to end. You'll start before the beginning -- with intelligences, spirit children, and the council in heaven where you chose to come to earth. You'll learn about the war in heaven, the Creation, the Fall, and why a Savior was needed. You'll understand how justice and mercy work together without either one being cheated. You'll follow the plan through earth life, the spirit world, the resurrection, the degrees of glory, the Millennium, and the final judgment.

And you'll learn what the plan means for you right now -- how ordinances and covenants help you progress, how being born again changes you, how the Savior makes up the difference when you fall short, and how families can be sealed together forever.

The plan of salvation is the most important thing you will ever learn. Everything else in the gospel -- the priesthood, the scriptures, the temple, the covenants -- exists because of this plan.

Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes. Read the lesson, talk it over with the AI tutor, then answer a few questions in your own words.
description: A comprehensive guide to the plan of salvation for Latter-day Saint kids. Covers intelligences, premortal life, the council and war in heaven, the Creation, the Fall, the need for a Savior, justice and mercy, the Atonement, being born again, the spirit world, temple work for the dead, the resurrection, degrees of glory, the Millennium, the final judgment, perfection through the Savior, and sealed families. Scripture references throughout.
what_youll_learn: Where you came from -- intelligences, spirit children, and the premortal life
The council in heaven, the war in heaven, and why you chose to come to earth
Why the earth was created and why the Fall was necessary
Why a Savior was needed and how justice and mercy work together
The Atonement and what it makes possible -- resurrection and repentance
How laws, covenants, and ordinances help you progress toward becoming like God
Being born again, joint heirs with Christ, and receiving all the Father hath
The spirit world, temple work for the dead, and how everyone gets a chance
The resurrection, three degrees of glory, perdition, and the final judgment
The Millennium, the celestial earth, and perfection through the Savior
expectations: 15 chapters, designed for one chapter per week (4 short lessons per week). Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes.
prerequisites: None. This is the foundational book of the series -- everything else builds on it.
tutor_instructions:
This book covers the complete plan of salvation for Latter-day Saint kids ages 7-12. It is the most foundational doctrinal book in the series.

When explaining and when grading:
- Use plain, simple words. Prefer short sentences.
- The plan is PERFECTLY JUST (every law has a consequence, justice is never violated), PERFECTLY MERCIFUL (the Savior paid the price so mercy can be extended), and PERFECTLY FAIR (everyone gets a chance through spirit world teaching and temple work). Reinforce these themes.
- Help kids see the plan as personal -- not abstract theology but THEIR story. They lived before. They chose to come. They have a purpose. They can return.
- The Atonement is the center of the plan. Everything before points to it. Everything after depends on it.
- Scripture references are included throughout. Encourage kids to look them up.
- When grading, accept any answer that captures the lesson's core idea in the student's own words.
- Be encouraging. The goal is to help kids understand the most important doctrine in the gospel and see their place in it.
